items.observation: Action target and Apply effect state persisted across actors, scenes, and encounters. Old off-scene actors remained selectable, and duplicate Cultist 1/Cultist 2 labels made stale and current targets indistinguishable.
items.impact: Credible wrong-target mutation risk.
items.workaround: Re-open the acting character, clear effect state, and identify the target from current-scene context before previewing.

items.observation: Consecutive invite creation produced revision conflicts.
items.impact: A GM cannot reliably create several invitations in one uninterrupted pass.

items.observation: One accepted player invitation later produced Unknown user session/401 behavior.
items.impact: The affected seat could not enter the campaign with the original invitation.
items.workaround: Issue and accept a fresh invitation.

items.observation: The encounter quantity control lost its accessible name after the first Add operation; reproduced in Sessions 2 and 3.
items.impact: Accessible automation and assistive technology cannot identify the increment control by role and name.
items.workaround: Count the remaining buttons and select the expected positional control.

items.observation: Generic damage was rejected in favor of reviewed typed damage, while direct HP editing and actor-summary HP showed conflicting feedback.
items.impact: The rules guard may be intentional, but it is unclear whether the requested change committed.
items.workaround: Use action preview, exact consequence review, and exact commit for typed damage, then verify the target sheet.

items.observation: An action preview opened a second consequence-confirmation modal; navigating elsewhere cancelled the reviewed action without a strong pending-action warning.
items.impact: A reviewed attack or spell can appear complete while no mutation is committed.
items.workaround: Remain in the flow through both confirmation steps and verify roll/damage persistence.

items.observation: A planned session saved as Unscheduled and the scheduling field was blank afterward.
items.impact: Planned session timing is not trustworthy from the visible confirmation state.
items.workaround: Treat the session as unscheduled and rely on title/number until timing is re-entered and verified.

items.observation: Generate session recap after Session 3 aggregated all three campaign combats rather than isolating Session 3.
items.impact: The session-specific label obscures campaign-wide recap scope.
items.workaround: Treat generated recap metrics as campaign totals.

items.observation: All three completed session records have empty encounterIds arrays even though three encounters and combats persisted.
items.impact: A session cannot be reconstructed through its encounter relationship alone.
items.workaround: Correlate session, scene, encounter, combat, and journal records by timestamps and narrative.

items.observation: All encounter tokenIds arrays remained empty even though monster scene tokens and combatants persisted.
items.impact: Encounter definitions do not retain direct token linkage from live play.
items.workaround: Use combatant actor/token references and scene contents for reconstruction.

items.observation: GM session reports retained their creation-time status instead of following the session to completed.
items.impact: Each report required a manual status correction after play.
items.workaround: Edit the structured report after completing the session.

items.observation: Session completion sometimes required a second click after roll/chat rerenders.
items.impact: The first click can look accepted without completing the session.
items.workaround: Wait for the rerender and confirm the completed status.

items.observation: The People count showed 1 despite four accepted player members.
items.impact: The visible count does not communicate the accepted campaign roster.

items.observation: Selecting a scene while Manage was open could close or redirect the workflow.
items.impact: Multi-map preparation required a non-obvious Prep, select, then Manage sequence.
items.workaround: Return to Prep before selecting the next scene, then reopen Manage.

items.observation: The active-session banner briefly showed the prior scene before realtime state converged.
items.impact: The GM can momentarily see stale scene context.
items.workaround: Wait for the live snapshot to settle and verify the active map.

items.observation: Every new scene required manual party-token placement; Aric was consequently absent from Furnace Bridge while the other three PCs were present.
items.impact: Repetitive setup creates omissions during multi-map sessions.
items.workaround: Use a per-scene four-PC placement checklist; all four were restored on the final map.

items.observation: Character creation was lengthy, and duplicate spell labels required group-scoped selection rather than unique accessible names.
items.impact: Four-character setup is slow and ambiguous for automation or assistive technology.
items.workaround: Scope spell selection to the correct group and validate the final prepared list.

items.observation: The web app initially connected to a retained Docker API on the expected port rather than the current-source API.
items.impact: Browser results could have exercised stale server code.
items.workaround: Rebuild the current API and verify its health before play.

items.observation: A file-picker timeout after selecting the wrong scene reset the automation kernel.
items.impact: Automation state was interrupted, though campaign state was not lost.
items.workaround: Reacquire the tab and continue from persisted product state.

items.observation: One operator controlled the DM and all four player accounts.
items.impact: No independent human usability, social-play, or coordination evidence was produced.

items.observation: Seat switching was sequential rather than genuinely concurrent.
items.impact: Realtime write conflicts and simultaneous presence behavior were not meaningfully exercised.

items.observation: Reload persistence was verified, but real reconnect, restore, staff repair, and failure-driven abandonment variants were not exercised.
items.impact: The run does not close recovery or retention evidence requirements.

items.observation: The run used one desktop Browser and one network context.
items.impact: No mixed-device, mobile, degraded-network, or accessibility-user evidence was produced.
